name: Modified low-N ASP-2, N-deprived, pure Ar sparge (no CO2) (PMC3419522)
description: Modified low-N ASP-2, N-deprived, pure Ar sparge (no CO2) — medium reported in PMC3419522, extracted for GrowthDB and mapped to BiGG.

provenance.citation: Medium composition from PMC3419522 (see GrowthDB record).
provenance.notes: Full recipe recovered from the primary literature: base medium 'Modified ASP-2 medium (van Baalen 1962) for Cyanothece sp. ATCC 51142; low-N / N-limited variant' (van Baalen C. 1962. Studies on marine blue-green algae. Botanica Marina 4:129-139 (cited as ref [34] in the paper). Base mineral-salt composition and the ASP-2 / ASP-2-N (nitrogen-free) variant taken from the ASP-2 recipe listing at cellreg.org (http://cellreg.org/Catalog_2020/Catalog%20NEW/media/ASP%202+ASP%202-N.html) which cites van
provenance.verification_evidence: "modified low-N ASP-2 containing 0.75 mM NH4Cl"; N-deprivation: "interruption of medium influx to the bioreactor" / "H2 became detectable within 1 h upon the perturbation"; gas: alternative condition with "pure Ar" atmosphere (no CO2).
provenance.recipe_evidence: From the paper (Methods): 'Cyanothece 51142 was routinely maintained in modified ASP-2 medium [34], containing 17 mM NH4Cl, 0.03 mM FeCl3, and 0.75 mM K2HPO4.' For the chemostat/H2-production runs: 'modified low-N ASP-2 containing 0.75 mM NH4Cl delivered at a 0.05 h-1 dilution rate and sparging with 1.3% CO2-Ar gas mix (vol/vol) at 4.08 liters/min'; a variant used 'pure Ar' to examine CO2 effects. Reference [34] = van Baalen C. 1962. Studies on marine blue-green algae. Bot. Mar. 4:129-139. Base ASP-2 mineral salts (van Baalen 1962, per liter): NaCl 18 g (308 mM), MgSO4.7H2O 0.75 g (3.0 mM), KC
provenance.base_medium: Modified ASP-2 medium (van Baalen 1962) for Cyanothece sp. ATCC 51142; low-N / N-limited variant
provenance.base_medium_reference: van Baalen C. 1962. Studies on marine blue-green algae. Botanica Marina 4:129-139 (cited as ref [34] in the paper). Base mineral-salt composition and the ASP-2 / ASP-2-N (nitrogen-free) variant taken from the ASP-2 recipe listing at cellreg.org (http://cellreg.org/Catalog_2020/Catalog%20NEW/media/ASP%202+ASP%202-N.html) which cites van Baalen 1962; P-II trace-metal solution from Provasoli, McLaughlin & Droop 1957 (P II metals, per 100 mL: Na2EDTA 100 mg, H3BO3 114 mg, FeCl3.6H2O 4.9 mg, MnSO4.H2O 16.4 mg, ZnSO4.7H2O 2.2 mg, CoSO4.7H2O 0.48 mg).

uncovered.name: dipotassium hydrogen phosphate
